Title: Molecular Evolution of Plant 14-3-3 Proteins and Function of Hv14-3-3A in Stomatal Regulation and Drought Tolerance

Plant_Genes.Notes_or_Other_Important_Info: Hv14-3-3A is located on barley chromosome 4H and shares high sequence similarity (>90%) with rice 14-3-3 orthologues. Its promoter contains multiple ABA- and stress-related cis-elements (ABRE, TGACG, CGTCA, LTR, GC-motif), consistent with strong drought responsiveness. Together with Hv14-3-3B and Hv14-3-3C, it is proposed as an important contributor to genotypic differences in drought tolerance between wild barley accessions XZ141 and XZ54.
Plant_Genes.Summary_key_Findings_of_Core_Gene:
1. Hv14-3-3A is one of six barley 14-3-3 genes, located on chromosome 4H and carrying conserved 14-3-3 motifs and multiple ABA- and stress-responsive cis-elements in its promoter.
2. Hv14-3-3A shows broad expression across barley tissues and is strongly drought inducible in leaves of the drought-tolerant genotype XZ141 but downregulated in the drought-sensitive genotype XZ54.
3. BSMV-VIGS silencing of Hv14-3-3A in barley seedlings (XZ141) greatly reduces Hv14-3-3A transcript levels and results in increased drought sensitivity, characterized by enhanced drought damage, increased stomatal density, elevated VPD, reduced net CO2 assimilation and stomatal conductance, and failure to increase iWUE under drought.
4. Hv14-3-3A physically interacts with key drought- and stomatal-related proteins HvOST1.1, HvOST1.5, HvSLAC1 and HSP90-1/2, indicating that it is a core component of the OST1–SLAC1 and HSP90-associated signaling modules for rapid stomatal responses to drought in barley.
Plant_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Silencing Hv14-3-3A decreases drought tolerance by increasing stomatal density and vapor pressure deficit and reducing net CO2 assimilation, stomatal conductance and intrinsic water use efficiency in barley leaves under drought.
Plant_Genes.Regulatory_Mechanism: Acts as a 14-3-3 scaffold protein that binds OST1 protein kinases, the anion channel SLAC1 and HSP90-1/2, suggesting modulation of OST1–SLAC1 signaling complexes and HSP90-associated chaperone functions in guard cells during drought.
Plant_Genes.Regulatory_Pathway: Functions in the ABA-dependent OST1–SLAC1 stomatal regulation pathway and associated drought-stress signaling network in barley guard cells.
Plant_Genes.Upstream_or_Downstream_Regulation: Hv14-3-3A --(protein–protein interaction)--> HvOST1.1; Hv14-3-3A --(protein–protein interaction)--> HvOST1.5; Hv14-3-3A --(protein–protein interaction)--> HvSLAC1; Hv14-3-3A --(protein–protein interaction)--> HvHSP90-1; Hv14-3-3A --(protein–protein interaction)--> HvHSP90-2; these interactions position Hv14-3-3A as a regulatory hub modulating OST1–SLAC1 and HSP90-mediated drought and stomatal signaling.
Plant_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: 14-3-3 protein (general regulatory factor) containing the canonical PF00244 14-3-3 domain with conserved internal binding space, phosphorylation site, annexin-like, PKC pseudosubstrate and nuclear export signal motifs.
Plant_Genes.Interacting_Proteins: HvOST1.1, HvOST1.5, HvSLAC1, HvHSP90-1 and HvHSP90-2 were shown to interact with Hv14-3-3A by LUC complementation assays in N. benthamiana leaves, and HvOST1.1–Hv14-3-3A interaction was further confirmed by yeast two-hybrid.
Plant_Genes.Expression_Pattern: Hv14-3-3A is predominantly expressed across examined barley tissues including inflorescences and roots, and shows high transcript levels compared with other Hv14-3-3s. Under drought, Hv14-3-3A transcripts are strongly induced in leaves of the drought-tolerant genotype XZ141 but reduced in the drought-sensitive genotype XZ54.
Plant_Genes.Key_Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Soil-drying drought stress applied for 3–10 days to potted barley seedlings after the two- to three-leaf stage; comparison to well-watered control conditions.
Plant_Genes.Research_Topic: Drought tolerance and stomatal regulation in barley through 14-3-3-mediated signaling.
Plant_Genes.Trait_Category: Abiotic stress tolerance (drought tolerance; stomatal conductance, stomatal density and photosynthetic performance under water deficit).
Plant_Genes.Quantitative_Phenotypic_Alterations: BSMV:Hv14-3-3A reduced Hv14-3-3A transcript levels in leaves by approximately 82% under control conditions and 84% after 10 days of drought; stomatal density in Hv14-3-3A-silenced leaves increased by about 17% relative to controls under drought.
Plant_Genes.Other_Phenotypic_Effects: Silencing Hv14-3-3A caused visible drought-induced damage in barley seedlings compared with controls, including reduced overall plant vigor under drought; control BSMV:γ plants increased intrinsic water use efficiency under drought whereas Hv14-3-3A-silenced plants failed to do so.
Plant_Genes.Experimental_Materials: Barley (Hordeum vulgare) Tibetan wild accessions XZ141 (drought tolerant) and XZ54 (drought sensitive); BSMV:Hv14-3-3A and BSMV:γ-inoculated XZ141 seedlings; barley leaves for RNA extraction and qRT-PCR; leaves of Nicotiana benthamiana for LUC assays; yeast strain AH109 for Y2H assays.
Plant_Genes.Experimental_Methods: Comparative expression analysis of Hv14-3-3s, HvOST1s and HvSLAC1 in leaves of contrasting barley genotypes under control and drought using qRT-PCR; BSMV-VIGS to silence Hv14-3-3A; drought treatment of potted seedlings; measurement of chlorophyll fluorescence (Fv/Fm), leaf temperature, stomatal density, and gas exchange parameters (A, E, Ci, gs, VPD, iWUE) with LI-6800; LUC-based protein–protein interaction assays in N. benthamiana; yeast two-hybrid assays for Hv14-3-3A–HvOST1.1 interaction; in silico motif, gene structure and cis-element analysis for Hv14-3-3 genes.
Plant_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: BSMV-mediated virus-induced gene silencing (BSMV-VIGS) of Hv14-3-3A in barley seedlings combined with qRT-PCR validation of knockdown, drought stress treatment, stomatal density and gas exchange measurements; protein–protein interaction assays (firefly luciferase complementation in Nicotiana benthamiana and yeast two-hybrid) with HvOST1s, HvSLAC1 and HvHSP90s.
Plant_Genes.Gene_Discovery_or_Cloning_Method: Hv14-3-3A was identified as one of six Hv14-3-3 genes by searching the barley genome (Ensembl Plants and NCBI) with the PFAM 14-3-3 domain (PF00244) using HMMER, followed by confirmation of conserved domains with SMART and NCBI CDD and naming based on previous studies and chromosomal location.
Plant_Genes.Breeding_Application_Value: Positive regulator of drought tolerance and water-use efficiency in barley seedlings; manipulating Hv14-3-3A expression or its interacting stomatal signaling partners (OST1–SLAC1, HSP90-1/2) could be used to breed barley lines with improved drought tolerance via optimized stomatal density and maintenance of photosynthesis under water deficit.
